title: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
summary: Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search after the European Union ordered the company to comply with the bloc's digital antitrust rules. The two decisions, handed down Thursday, could weaken Google's control over two of the tech industry's most important platforms and have […]
title_es: UE ordena a Google abrir Android y Search a competidores
bajada: La Unión Europea obligó a Google a dar mayor acceso a asistentes IA rivales y motores de búsqueda en Android y Google Search. Las decisiones podrían debilitar significativamente el control de Google sobre dos de las plataformas más importantes de la industria.
bajada_en: The EU ordered Google to grant rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to Android and Google Search under digital antitrust rules. The decisions could significantly weaken Google's control over two critical industry platforms.

title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
summary: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.
title_es: OpenAI presenta GPT-Red, sistema de red teaming automatizado
bajada: OpenAI lanzó GPT-Red, un sistema que usa auto-juego para mejorar la seguridad y robustez de sus modelos contra inyección de prompts. Esta aproximación automatiza las pruebas de adversarios, permitiendo identificar vulnerabilidades de forma más eficiente.
bajada_en: OpenAI unveiled GPT-Red, a system using self-play to improve model safety and robustness against prompt injection attacks. This automated red teaming approach makes vulnerability detection more efficient than traditional adversarial testing.

title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
summary: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.
title_es: Apple Intelligence llega a China con Alibaba y Baidu
bajada: Apple recibió aprobación para lanzar Apple Intelligence en China usando modelos de Alibaba (Qwen) y Baidu. Este acuerdo marca un paso importante para las ambiciones de IA de Apple en su segundo mercado más grande.
bajada_en: Apple received approval to launch Apple Intelligence in China using models from Alibaba and Baidu. This deal represents a critical step for Apple's AI strategy in one of its largest markets.

title: NVIDIA Introduces New Jetson Thor Computers to Advance Mainstream Robotics and Edge AI
summary: General-purpose robots and autonomous machines are moving from research labs to real-world mass-market deployment, creating demand for compact, power-efficient AI supercomputers capable of running foundation models at the edge.  To meet that need, NVIDIA today introduced the T3000 and T2000, new modules based on the NVIDIA Thor architecture that enable mass-market robotics and edge AI […]
title_es: NVIDIA presenta Jetson Thor para robótica y IA en edge
bajada: NVIDIA anunció los módulos T3000 y T2000 basados en arquitectura Thor para llevar IA y robótica a producción masiva. Estos sistemas compactos y eficientes permitirán ejecutar foundation models directamente en robots y máquinas autónomas.
bajada_en: NVIDIA introduced the T3000 and T2000 modules based on Thor architecture to enable mass-market robotics and edge AI deployment. These compact, power-efficient systems will run foundation models directly on robots and autonomous machines.

title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
summary: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
title_es: OpenAI propone federalismo inverso para gobernar la IA en EE.UU.
bajada: OpenAI esbozó una estrategia de "federalismo inverso" donde leyes estatales ayuden a construir un marco nacional para IA segura. La propuesta busca equilibrar innovación con gobernanza democrática en diferentes jurisdicciones.
bajada_en: OpenAI outlined a 'reverse federalism' approach where state laws help build a national AI governance framework. The proposal aims to balance innovation with democratic oversight across jurisdictions.

title: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
summary: 1Password has launched a new browser integration for Claude that allows the Anthropic chatbot to access stored security credentials like usernames and passwords. The 1Password for Claude feature means that users can authorize Claude to complete multi-step tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on their behalf without having to manually input their login […]
title_es: Claude ahora puede acceder a credenciales de 1Password
bajada: 1Password integró sus credenciales con Claude, permitiendo al chatbot completar tareas multi-paso como reservas de viajes sin introducción manual de datos. Esta integración amplía las capacidades de agentes pero plantea nuevas consideraciones de seguridad.
bajada_en: 1Password integrated with Claude, allowing the chatbot to access stored credentials and complete multi-step tasks like travel bookings automatically. This extends agent capabilities while raising new security considerations.

title: Thinking Machines Lab Drops Its First Model
summary: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.
title_es: Thinking Machines Lab lanza Inkling, modelo multimodal de 975B
bajada: Thinking Machines Lab liberó Inkling, un modelo open-source de 975 mil millones de parámetros entrenado en video y audio. El lanzamiento posiciona a la startup como competidor serio frente a Anthropic y OpenAI.
bajada_en: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open-source model trained on video and audio. The launch establishes the startup as a serious competitor to Anthropic and OpenAI.

title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
summary: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.
title_es: Applied Computing recauda $20M para IA en petróleo y gas
bajada: Applied Computing aseguró $20M en Serie A para desarrollar modelos de IA especializados en la industria de petróleo, gas y petroquímica. La ronda financia la creación de soluciones verticalizadas para operadores de plantas.
bajada_en: Applied Computing raised $20M Series A to build specialized AI foundation models for the oil, gas, and petrochemical industry. The funding supports vertical AI solutions for plant operators.

title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception.This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what drives the choice, what they optimize for, how they expect agent control to be structured, and — most revealingly — how orchestrated their deployed “agents” actually are and how tightly they control the cost of running them.The central finding is a gap between orchestration ambition and orchestration reality. Enterprises are consolidating fast onto the major model platforms: Anthropic’s Claude is the primary platform for 40%, more than double any rival, followed by Microsoft (18%) and OpenAI (13%). The choice is driven by “model gravity” — native alignment with a state-of-the-art base model (21%) — and success is judged by reliable, multi-step execution (task completion reliability 32%, multi-step workflow management 28%). Yet asked to assess their portfolios honestly, 71% say a quarter or fewer of their deployed “agents” are true multi-step orchestrated workflows rather than single-prompt chatbot wrappers, and only 10% have crossed the halfway mark. The orchestration layer is being built well ahead of the orchestrated portfolio it is meant to run.That gap shapes the architecture enterprises are putting in place. By the end of 2026 a clear majority (51%) expect a hybrid control plane — provider-native plus external orchestration — and only 6% expect to hand control to a provider-managed service, because vendor lock-in (35%) is the risk they fear most if control lives inside a model
title_es: Empresas confunden chatbots con agentes reales de IA
bajada: Investigación de VentureBeat revela que la mayoría de empresas despliega "agentes" que son en realidad chatbots envueltos, no sistemas orquestados. Claude lidera con 40% de adopción, pero existe brecha entre ambiciones de orquestación y realidad.
bajada_en: VentureBeat research shows most enterprises deploy 'agents' that are actually wrapped chatbots, not true orchestrated systems. Claude leads with 40% adoption, but a gap exists between orchestration ambition and reality.

summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. bajada: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest ver

title: Security incident disclosure — July 2026
title_es: Hugging Face divulga incidente de seguridad de julio 2026
bajada: Hugging Face reportó un incidente de seguridad sin detalles públicos en el anuncio. La divulgación es parte del compromiso de transparencia en la comunidad open-source.
bajada_en: Hugging Face disclosed a security incident from July 2026 with limited public details. The disclosure reflects the community's commitment to transparency.

title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.
title_es: Microsoft entrena vendedores para competir contra OpenAI
bajada: Microsoft está capacitando a su fuerza de ventas para posicionar sus modelos de IA propios como más eficientes y económicos que los de OpenAI y Anthropic. La estrategia refleja competencia creciente en el mercado de modelos.
bajada_en: Microsoft is training salespeople to position its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than OpenAI and Anthropic's offerings. The strategy reflects intensifying competition in the model marketplace.

title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whether their defenses are keeping pace with AI-enabled attackers. The result is an agent security gap — autonomous agents proliferating faster than the identity, isolation, and enforcement controls needed to hold them.This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines how enterprises secure their AI agents: what tooling they run, how they manage agent identity and isolation, what has already gone wrong, how much they spend, and whether they believe their defenses are keeping pace with AI-enabled attackers.The central finding is an agent security gap — the distance between the autonomy enterprises are granting their agents and the controls in place to contain them. More than half of organizations (54%) have already experienced a confirmed agent security incident (18%) or a near-miss caught before harm (36%). The structural weakness beneath those numbers is identity: only about a third (32%) give every agent its own scoped, managed identity, while the rest report that some agents share credentials or that agents mostly run on shared API keys and human or service-account credentials. When agents share credentials, a single compromised or over-permissioned agent carries a wide blast radius — and only three in ten enterprises (30%) isolate their highest-risk agents in sandboxes to bound that radius.What makes the gap notable is how comfortable enterprises are inside it. The security stack
title_es: 54% de empresas ya sufrió incidentes de seguridad con agentes
bajada: Un estudio de VentureBeat revela que más del 50% de empresas experimentó incidentes de seguridad con agentes de IA, mientras que controles de aislamiento y credenciales aún quedan rezagados. Expone una brecha crítica en la madurez de seguridad agentica empresarial.
bajada_en: VentureBeat research reveals over 50% of enterprises suffered AI agent security incidents, while isolation and credential controls remain immature. It exposes a critical gap in enterprise agentic security maturity.

title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider-native tools lead in practice, a plurality say they intend to keep best-of-breed. The result is a context gap — agents that sound authoritative running on a foundation their owners do not yet fully trust.This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines the enterprise RAG and context layer: what feeds AI agents their business context, which retrieval systems enterprises run, how they buy and measure them, where the architecture is heading, and — most revealingly — how often that context is already failing them.The central finding is a context gap — the distance between how confidently enterprise agents answer and how reliable the context beneath them actually is. A majority of enterprises (57%) report that in the past six months their AI agents produced confident but wrong answers they traced to missing or inconsistent business context, and more than half of those said it happened more than once. This is not a fringe failure: retrieval is the primary context source for 38% of enterprises, more than any other approach, so when retrieval is thin or inconsistent, the errors it produces are wearing the agent’s authority. The infrastructure to fix it is being built — 58% already run or are building a governed semantic layer — but for most it is not yet in production.Underneath, the market is consolidating in a direction that surprises. Provider-native retrieval — OpenAI’s file searc
title_es: La brecha de contexto: empresas luchan con confianza en RAG
bajada: Un estudio de VentureBeat en 101 empresas muestra que la mayoría construye infraestructura de contexto más rápido de lo que puede confiar en ella. La mayoría ya presenció agentes que producen respuestas confiantes pero incorrectas debido a contexto inconsistente.
bajada_en: VentureBeat research across 101 enterprises shows most build context infrastructure faster than they can trust it. Most already witnessed agents producing confident but incorrect answers due to inconsistent context.

title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap — the distance between how much autonomy enterprises are handing their agents and how far they trust the tests that are supposed to catch the failures.This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines how technical leaders measure agent performance: which reliability and evaluation platforms they use, how they select and trust them, what breaks in production, and how far they are willing to let agents run without a human in the loop.The central finding is an evaluation gap — the distance between the autonomy enterprises are granting their agents and the trust they place in the evaluations meant to govern it. Half of organizations (50%) have, in the past year, deployed an agent or LLM feature that passed their internal evaluations and then caused a customer-facing failure, and a quarter have seen it happen more than once. Trust in the tests themselves is thin: only 5% say they fully trust automated evaluation today, and the single most-cited limitation is that evaluations align poorly with real-world outcomes (29%). Enterprises are discovering that a passing eval is not the same as a working agent.What makes the gap consequential is the direction of travel. Two-thirds of organizations (66%) already permit fully automated, zero-human-in-the-loop deployment for low-risk agents (34%) or are actively engineering their pipelines to allow it within twelve months (33%). At the same time,
title_es: Brecha de evaluación: empresas despliegan agentes sin validación total
bajada: Un estudio de VentureBeat en 157 empresas revela que el 50% ya desplegó agentes que pasaron evaluaciones internas pero fallaron en producción. Dos tercios permiten cambios agenticos en producción sin intervención humana, a pesar de desconfiar de evaluaciones automatizadas.
bajada_en: VentureBeat research across 157 enterprises shows 50% already deployed agents that passed internal evaluations but failed in production. Two-thirds allow agent changes to production without human oversight despite distrusting automated evaluations.

title: How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
summary: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.
title_es: Cars24 escala a 1M de minutos mensuales con agentes de OpenAI
bajada: Cars24 implementó agentes de voz y chat impulsados por OpenAI para manejar más de 1 millón de minutos de conversación al mes, recuperando el 12% de clientes perdidos. El caso de uso demuestra cómo los workflows agenticos están transformando operaciones empresariales reales a escala.
bajada_en: Cars24 deployed OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle over 1M monthly conversation minutes, recovering 12% of lost leads. The use case demonstrates how agentic workflows are transforming real business operations at scale.

title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed Ranks #1 Overall on RTEB, Advancing Agentic Retrieval
title_es: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed lidera en RTEB para retrieval agentico
bajada: El nuevo modelo embedding de NVIDIA alcanzó el primer lugar en el benchmark RTEB, mejorando el rendimiento de sistemas de recuperación para agentes. Esto marca un progreso importante en la infraestructura de retrieval specializada para aplicaciones agenticas.
bajada_en: NVIDIA's new embedding model ranked #1 on the RTEB benchmark, advancing retrieval performance for agentic systems. This marks significant progress in specialized retrieval infrastructure for agentic applications.

title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
summary: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.
title_es: DoorDash lanza dd-cli para ordenes desde la terminal
bajada: DoorDash abrió una beta limitada de dd-cli, una herramienta de línea de comandos que permite a desarrolladores y agentes de IA buscar tiendas, construir carritos y hacer pedidos. Representa un cambio importante hacia software diseñado para interactuar con agentes de IA.
bajada_en: DoorDash launched a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool enabling developers and AI agents to search stores, build carts, and place orders. It represents a shift toward software designed for AI agent interaction.

title: German AI consortium releases Soofi S, an open 30B model that tops benchmarks
summary: Article URL:https://the-decoder.com/german-ai-consortium-releases-soofi-s-an-open-30b-model-that-tops-benchmarks-in-both-english-and-german/Comments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48937756Points: 111# Comments: 25
title_es: Consorcio alemán libera Soofi S, modelo open de 30B parámetros
bajada: Un consorcio de IA alemán lanzó Soofi S, un modelo open-source de 30B parámetros que encabeza benchmarks en inglés y alemán. Demuestra el avance de modelos de código abierto competitivos a escala media.
bajada_en: A German AI consortium released Soofi S, a 30B parameter open-source model topping benchmarks in English and German. It demonstrates the advance of competitive open-source models at mid-scale.

title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
summary: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.
title_es: Google Vids agrega avatares de IA personalizados
bajada: Google expandió Vids con avatares de IA personalizados que permiten a usuarios crear videos protagonizados por versiones digitales de sí mismos. La herramienta se integra con Gemini Omni para generación y edición de videos desde prompts.
bajada_en: Google expanded Vids with personalized AI avatars letting users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. The tool integrates Gemini Omni for video generation and editing from prompts.

title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
summary: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.
title_es: AI Mode de Google ahora integra apps seleccionadas
bajada: Google expandió AI Mode para completar tareas dentro de aplicaciones integradas, más allá de solo responder preguntas. El update muestra la evolución hacia agentes de IA que actúan en aplicaciones reales.
bajada_en: Google expanded AI Mode to complete tasks across integrated apps, moving beyond question-answering. The update shows evolution toward AI agents acting on real applications.

title: LM Studio Bionic: the AI agent for open models
summary: Article URL:https://lmstudio.ai/blog/introducing-lm-studio-bionicComments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48939662Points: 83# Comments: 27
title_es: LM Studio Bionic: agente de IA para modelos open-source
bajada: LM Studio presentó Bionic, un agente de IA diseñado específicamente para trabajar con modelos open-source. Ofrece a developers una opción local para ejecutar agentes agenticos sin depender de APIs cerradas.
bajada_en: LM Studio introduced Bionic, an AI agent designed specifically for open-source models. It offers developers a local option to run agentic workflows without relying on closed APIs.

title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
summary: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.
title_es: Roblox lanza creación de juegos con IA en móvil
bajada: Roblox agregó una nueva feature "Build" que permite a usuarios generar juegos básicos a partir de un prompt de texto. Democratiza la creación de juegos dentro de su plataforma móvil.
bajada_en: Roblox added a new "Build" feature letting users generate basic games from a text prompt. It democratizes game creation within its mobile platform.

title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
summary: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.
title_es: OpenAI implementa protecciones de IA para adolescentes
bajada: OpenAI anunció medidas de seguridad específicas para adolescentes en ChatGPT, incluyendo controles parentales y protecciones apropiadas por edad. Refleja el enfoque de la compañía en acceso seguro a IA para menores.
bajada_en: OpenAI announced teen-specific safety measures for ChatGPT, including parental controls and age-appropriate protections. It reflects the company's focus on safe AI access for minors.

title: Connect more of your apps to Search
summary: Connected apps rendering
title_es: Google conecta más apps a Search para agentics
bajada: Google expandió Connected Apps en Search para que agentes de IA accedan a más servicios integrados. Amplía la capacidad de agentes para interactuar con ecosistemas de aplicaciones.
bajada_en: Google expanded Connected Apps in Search to let AI agents access more integrated services. It expands agent capability to interact with application ecosystems.

title: Detecting LLM-Generated Texts with “Classical” Machine Learning
summary: Article URL:https://blog.lyc8503.net/en/post/llm-classifier/Comments URL:https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48936880Points: 127# Comments: 92
title_es: Detectar textos generados por LLM con machine learning clásico
bajada: Un investigador demostró que técnicas de machine learning clásico pueden detectar textos generados por LLMs de forma efectiva. Ofrece alternativas a métodos de detección complejos para identificar contenido sintético.
bajada_en: Researcher demonstrated that classical machine learning techniques can effectively detect LLM-generated texts. It offers alternatives to complex detection methods for identifying synthetic content.

title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
summary: Google is giving its AI note-taking app a new name. The company announced on Thursday that NotebookLM is becoming Gemini Notebook, but will remain a standalone app even as it integrates more deeply across Gemini and Google Search. Google first revealed Gemini Notebook - then called Project Tailwind - in May 2023 before widely releasing […]
title_es: Google renombra NotebookLM a Gemini Notebook
bajada: Google rebautizó NotebookLM como Gemini Notebook y lo mantiene como app standalone con integración más profunda con Gemini y Google Search. Es un cambio de marca para alinear la herramienta con su ecosistema Gemini.
bajada_en: Google rebranded NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook while keeping it standalone with deeper Gemini and Google Search integration. It's a rebranding to align the tool with its Gemini ecosystem.
